### Fix 1
Line: 74
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: control

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:

```
   74            <Button isDisabled={addingIsDisabled} onClick={onAdd} variant="control">
   75              {t('Add')}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 52
Rule [family:Label]:
## Label Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Label closeBtn={...} closeBtnAriaLabel={...} closeBtnProps={...} color={...} editableProps={...} href={...} icon={...} isClickable={...} />
```

Props that stay on <Label>: closeBtn, closeBtnAriaLabel, closeBtnProps, color, editableProps, href, icon, isClickable, isCompact, isDisabled, isEditable, onClick, onClose, onEditCancel, onEditComplete, render, status, textMaxWidth, tooltipPosition, variant

Prop type changes:
  color: 'blue' | 'cyan' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'grey' | 'gold' -> 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  color: 'blue' | 'cyan' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'grey' | 'gold' -> 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' -> 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' -> 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
New props on replacement:
  closeBtn: React.ReactNode
  closeBtnProps: any
  color: 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  editableProps: any
  href: string
  icon: React.ReactNode
  isClickable: boolean
  isCompact: boolean
  isDisabled: boolean
  isEditable: boolean
  onClose: (event: React.MouseEvent) => void
  onEditCancel: (event: KeyboardEvent, previousText: string) => void
  onEditComplete: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent, newText: string) => void
  render: ({
    className,
    content,
    componentRef
  }: {
    className: string;
    content: React.ReactNode;
    componentRef: any;
  }) => React.ReactNode
  status: 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'info' | 'custom'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): badge, component, isOverflowChip, isReadOnly, ouiaId

Incidents found in this file:
  Line 52: [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-label-label-labelprops-d-ts-label-variant-type-changed]
    componentName: Label
    module: @patternfly/react-core
    propName: variant
    propValue: outline
    Fix strategy:
      Strategy: PropTypeChange
      Component: Label
      Prop: variant
      From: 'filled' | 'outline'
      To: 'add' | 'filled' | 'outline' | 'overflow'

Code contexts:
  (line 52):
   51          {values.map((value) => (
   52            <Label id={value} key={value} onClose={() => onClose(value)} variant="outline">
   53              {value}



Code context:
```
(no snippet)
```

### Fix 3
Line: 74
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: InputGroupItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   73          <InputGroupItem>
   74            <Button isDisabled={addingIsDisabled} onClick={onAdd} variant="control">
   75              {t('Add')}

```

### Fix 4
Line: 52
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-label]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Label> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Label> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Label variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Label>
  After:  <Label variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Label> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Label><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Label>
  After:  <Label icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Label>

Incident context:
  componentName: Label
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: LabelGroup

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Label
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   51          {values.map((value) => (
   52            <Label id={value} key={value} onClose={() => onClose(value)} variant="outline">
   53              {value}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/AffinityModal/components/AffinityEditModal/AffinityForm/components/AffinityEditRowValues.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Line 74):** `Button.variant` type changed — but `variant="control"` is still valid in the new type (`'control'` is in both old and new union). No code change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 52):** `Label.variant` type changed — but `variant="outline"` is still valid in the new type (`'outline'` is in both old and new union). No code change needed.

**Fix 3 (Line 74):** Icon children to `icon` prop for `<Button>`. Looking at the code:
```jsx
<Button isDisabled={addingIsDisabled} onClick={onAdd} variant="control">
  {t('Add')}
</Button>
```
The Button contains only `{t('Add')}` which is text, NOT an icon. There is no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` here. No change needed.

**Fix 4 (Line 52):** Icon children to `icon` prop for `<Label>`. Looking at the code:
```jsx
<Label id={value} key={value} onClose={() => onClose(value)} variant="outline">
  {value}
</Label>
```
The Label contains only `{value}` which is a string variable, NOT an icon element. There is no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` here. No change needed.

All four fixes target code that is already valid — the prop values are still in the accepted union types, and there are no icon elements being passed as children that need to be moved to the `icon` prop.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button variant="control" type change): **No change needed** — `"control"` is a valid value in both the old and new type union.

2. **Fix 2** (Label variant="outline" type change): **No change needed** — `"outline"` is a valid value in both the old and new type union.

3. **Fix 3** (Button icon children to prop): **No change needed** — the Button's children is `{t('Add')}` (text), not an icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

4. **Fix 4** (Label icon children to prop): **No change needed** — the Label's children is `{value}` (a string variable), not an icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

**Fix 1 (Line 74):** `Button.variant` type changed — but `variant="control"` is still valid in the new type (`'control'` is in both old and new union). No code change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 52):** `Label.variant` type changed — but `variant="outline"` is still valid in the new type (`'outline'` is in both old and new union). No code change needed.

**Fix 3 (Line 74):** Icon children to `icon` prop for `<Button>`. Looking at the code:

```jsx
  {t('Add')}
</Button>
```

The Button contains only `{t('Add')}` which is text, NOT an icon. There is no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` here. No change needed.

**Fix 4 (Line 52):** Icon children to `icon` prop for `<Label>`. Looking at the code:

2. **Fix 2** (Label variant="outline" type change): **No change needed** — `"outline"` is a valid value in both the old and new type union.
